input_row.title: Ensure incoming client certificates are enabled and required (if in use)
input_row.remediation: Remediate from Azure Portal 1. Go to App Services or Function App. 2. Click the name of a function app. 3. Under Settings, click Configuration. 4. In the General settings pane, under Incoming client certificates, set Client certificate mode to Required. 5. Click Save. 6. Click Continue. 7. Repeat steps 1-6 for each function app requiring remediation. Remediate from Azure CLI For each function app requiring remediation, run the following command to enable and require incoming client certificates: az resource update --resource-group <resource-group-name> --name <function- app-name> --resource-type "Microsoft.Web/sites" --set properties.clientCertEnabled=true --set properties.clientCertMode=Required Default Value: By default, incoming client certificates are disabled. References: 1. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/security-controls-v3- identity-management#im-4-authenticate-server-and-services 2. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-gb/azure/app-service/app-service-web-configure- tls-mutual-auth 3. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cli/azure/functionapp 4. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cli/azure/resource CIS Controls:

gpt_response:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
The audit approach for ensuring that incoming client certificates are enabled and required is categorized as Automated because:

- The remediation section includes specific commands for the Azure CLI, which indicates that the control can be verified programmatically.
- The Azure Resource Manager APIs can support querying the properties of App Services and Function Apps, specifically the `clientCertEnabled` and `clientCertMode` properties.
- This control pertains to a standard configuration setting of Azure resources that can be audited using available Azure APIs.

**PROGRAM NAME:** azure_function_app_incoming_client_certificates_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:** 
The automated program would perform the following checks:

- Use Azure Resource Manager APIs or Azure CLI commands to enumerate Function Apps within specified resource groups.
- The CLI command to validate the client certificate mode settings would be as follows:
  ```bash
  az functionapp show --resource-group <resource-group-name> --name <function-app-name>
  ```
- The specific configuration properties to validate would be:
  - `properties.clientCertEnabled` should be `true`
  - `properties.clientCertMode` should be `Required`
- The clear Pass/Fail criteria would be:
  - Pass: if both of the above properties are as expected (i.e., `clientCertEnabled=true` and `clientCertMode=Required`)
  - Fail: if either property does not meet the expected values
- Example API endpoint for querying these properties could be constructed using the Azure REST API format to fetch details about Function Apps:
  ```
  GET https://management.azure.com/subscriptions/{subscription-id}/resourceGroups/{resource-group-name}/providers/Microsoft.Web/sites/{function-app-name}?api-version=2021-02-01
  ```
This automated approach provides a systematic and efficient means of verifying that incoming client certificates are configured as required across all specified Function Apps.
